name: Autologous Mitochondrial Transplantation
summary: Procedural transfer of viable mitochondria isolated from a patient's own nonischemic tissue into an injured or ischemic target organ.
mechanism_summary: The approach aims to isolate respiration-competent mitochondria from the recipient, then deliver them by local injection, intracoronary or intra-arterial infusion, or endovascular microcatheter delivery so injured tissue can take up functional mitochondria and partially restore bioenergetic capacity.

open_questions: Whether safety and feasibility scale beyond specialized acute ischemia settings
Whether target-tissue mitochondrial uptake produces durable functional benefit in controlled human trials
How dosing, delivery route, mitochondrial quality control, and tissue targeting should be standardized
Whether autologous sourcing is practical for chronic aging-relevant indications
